fresh_facts.claim: The US announced deployment of approximately 2,500 Marines and a Navy amphibious assault ship to the Middle East on March 13-14, 2026.
US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth stated that over 15,000 targets have been struck in Iran during bombing campaigns that began February 28, 2026.
13 US military personnel have been killed during operations related to strikes on Iran.
President Trump confirmed US bombing of military sites on Kharg Island, a key Iranian oil export hub, on March 13, 2026.
The Vermont Air National Guard's 158th Fighter Wing (F-35 units) is heading to the Middle East for operations in Iran.
President Trump stated that US strikes have decimated most of Iran's missiles and drones.
Joint US-Israeli bombing operations continue against Iran's regime and infrastructure, including hits on oil-related military sites.
Iran persists in attacking Persian Gulf shipping and energy infrastructure despite US and Israeli strikes.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei died prior to March 1, 2026.
On March 1, 2026, top Iranian security official Ali Larijani announced an Interim Leadership Council and warned secessionist groups.
Iran named Mojtaba Khamenei (Ali Khamenei's son) as the new Supreme Leader by March 9, 2026.
Mojtaba Khamenei issued his first statement as Supreme Leader on March 12, 2026.
A leadership rift emerged as Iranian President Pezeshkian apologized for Iranian strikes on Gulf states and ordered a halt, but Revolutionary Guards persisted.
US-Israeli strikes targeted Iranian internal security infrastructure on March 13, 2026, degrading regime capabilities.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: China supported Iran on March 1-2, 2026, urging the US and Israel to cease operations.
Pakistan's president condemned attacks on Iran and urged restraint around March 1, 2026.
US-Israeli strikes hit Iranian fuel depots, refineries, and desalination plants by March 9, 2026.
EU Commission President von der Leyen called for a credible transition in Iran, signaling regime change support.
French President Macron convened a security council regarding the Iran situation around March 1, 2026.
Italian Prime Minister Meloni opposed Iranian missile strikes in March 2026.
The US deployed the largest Middle East military buildup since 2003, triggered by Iran's nuclear program, protests, and pre-war tensions.
IRGC Aerospace Force conducted drills on February 26, 2026, simulating strikes on UAE's Al Dhafra base.
Satellite imagery showed accelerated missile site repairs over Iranian nuclear facilities as of February 26, 2026.
A 2025-2026 Iranian regime crackdown killed thousands of protesters prior to the current war.

regime_fall.current_status: US-Israeli bombing campaign has struck over 15,000 Iranian targets across critical infrastructure including oil facilities and internal security sites, with 2,500 Marines and F-35 units deployed, while the newly-installed Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei faces visible rifts between President Pezeshkian and the Revolutionary Guards over operational strategy. Economic infrastructure degradation, international diplomatic isolation, and emerging civil-military command fractures signal accelerating systemic strain despite regime continuity efforts.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth confirmed over 15,000 targets struck in Iran since February 28, 2026, with President Trump confirming strikes on Kharg Island oil infrastructure and stating Iranian missile and drone capabilities have been decimated as of March 13, 2026.
